title: NoSQL Document Store (DynamoDB)
description: AWS DynamoDB is a fully-managed serverless NoSQL database. Offers single-digit millisecond latency, automatic scaling, and global tables. Best for: high-throughput applications, serverless architectures, key-based access patterns. Optimized for simple queries with well-designed partition keys.

provides.reasoning: DynamoDB is fundamentally a NoSQL database service, providing non-relational data storage as its primary function.
DynamoDB natively stores data as JSON-like documents with flexible schema, supporting complex nested attributes and data structures up to 400KB per item.
DynamoDB primarily operates as a key-value store using partition keys and optional sort keys for data access, which is its core access pattern.
DynamoDB is specifically designed to provide single-digit millisecond response times for read and write operations at any scale through its distributed architecture and SSD storage.
DynamoDB Global Tables provides fully managed multi-region, multi-active replication with automatic conflict resolution and synchronization across AWS regions.
DynamoDB provides automatic scaling of read and write throughput capacity based on demand, eliminating the need for manual capacity planning.
DynamoDB is a fully-managed serverless database service where AWS handles all infrastructure, patching, and operational tasks.

requires.reasoning: DynamoDB is an AWS service and requires an active AWS account to provision and use the database service.
DynamoDB requires AWS IAM roles and policies to control access, authentication, and authorization for all database operations.

consistency_level.description: Read consistency model. Eventual=lower latency and cost, better availability. Strong=guaranteed up-to-date reads, higher latency and cost (2x read capacity)

billing_mode.description: Billing model. PAY_PER_REQUEST=serverless on-demand pricing, no capacity planning. PROVISIONED=reserved capacity with auto-scaling, cost-effective for predictable workloads

point_in_time_recovery.description: Enable continuous backups with 35-day retention for point-in-time recovery. Adds ~20% to storage costs but provides protection against accidental deletes/writes

encryption_at_rest.description: Encrypt data at rest using AWS KMS. Recommended for compliance (HIPAA, PCI-DSS). Minimal performance impact, adds KMS costs

global_tables.description: Enable multi-region replication for global applications. Provides low-latency local reads/writes and disaster recovery. Increases costs (replicated storage and cross-region transfer)

stream_enabled.description: Enable DynamoDB Streams to capture item-level changes. Required for event-driven architectures, CDC patterns, Lambda triggers, and real-time analytics

ttl_enabled.description: Enable Time-To-Live to automatically delete expired items. Useful for session data, temporary records, and compliance requirements. No additional cost

service_auth_method.description: DynamoDB uses AWS IAM for access control. 'iam_role' (EC2/ECS/Lambda execution role) is strongly preferred over static IAM user credentials.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: DynamoDB pricing based on: (1) Min $0: AWS Free Tier includes 25GB storage, 25 WCU, 25 RCU (sufficient for small apps, prototypes, and low-traffic applications with <200K requests/month). (2) Max $500: Represents a moderate production workload with ~500GB storage ($125), provisioned capacity of 100 WCU + 300 RCU ($140), on-demand backup ($50), and Point-in-Time Recovery ($100). Higher traffic applications (millions of requests) or global tables could reach $1000-$5000+, but $500 represents a typical mid-sized application baseline.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity adoption. Key factors: (1) Learning curve for NoSQL data modeling paradigm shift from relational databases (single-table design, partition key optimization). (2) Schema design requires upfront planning for access patterns to avoid expensive scans. (3) Understanding capacity modes (on-demand vs provisioned), GSI/LSI strategies, and DynamoDB Streams. (4) Integration with AWS SDK and IAM permissions setup. (5) Testing and optimization for hot partition issues. Estimated 40-60 hours of learning, design, and initial implementation for a team new to DynamoDB.
provenance.licenseCost: AWS DynamoDB is a fully-managed cloud service with pay-as-you-go pricing. No upfront license fees, no software to purchase. Costs are purely consumption-based (storage, throughput, features). Free tier available permanently for qualifying usage. No vendor lock-in licensing costs, though AWS account and payment method required.

supports_nfr.reason: DynamoDB with Global Tables provides up to 99.999% availability across multiple regions.
DynamoDB typically achieves single-digit millisecond latency with a floor around 10ms for p95.
DynamoDB p99 latency floor is around 50ms accounting for network overhead and occasional throttling.
DynamoDB as a managed service enables rapid recovery with automatic failover achieving RTO of at least 1 minute.
DynamoDB supports extremely high read query throughput with auto-scaling up to the platform ceiling.
DynamoDB supports extremely high write query throughput with auto-scaling up to the platform ceiling.
DynamoDB integrates with CloudTrail for audit logging which can be enabled or disabled.
DynamoDB supports all tenant isolation models from row-level with partition keys to per-tenant tables.
DynamoDB supports PII storage with encryption at rest via KMS and encryption in transit.
DynamoDB supports GDPR compliance with encryption, access controls, and data residency options.
DynamoDB is HIPAA eligible with encryption at rest and in transit and BAA support available.
DynamoDB supports CCPA requirements through fine-grained access control, encryption, and data deletion capabilities.
DynamoDB provides audit trails via CloudTrail, encryption, and access controls needed for SOX compliance.
DynamoDB supports both strongly consistent reads for read-your-writes and eventually consistent reads.
DynamoDB provides 11 nines durability through automatic replication across multiple availability zones.

supports_constraints.reason: DynamoDB is an AWS-specific managed service available only on Amazon Web Services.
DynamoDB is a database infrastructure pattern accessible via AWS SDKs available in all major languages.
DynamoDB is a backend database service that can be accessed from any platform type.
DynamoDB is a document-oriented NoSQL database that stores JSON-like documents with flexible schema.
DynamoDB can be used with or without an additional caching layer like DAX or ElastiCache.
DynamoDB works with or without async messaging and supports DynamoDB Streams for event-driven patterns.
DynamoDB can store data for AI inference workloads or general-purpose applications.
DynamoDB supports both single-tenant and multi-tenant architectures with partition key strategies.
DynamoDB supports both real-time and batch processing workloads with BatchGetItem and BatchWriteItem APIs.
DynamoDB supports ACID transactions within a single region and can be used in distributed transaction patterns.
DynamoDB Streams enables real-time streaming of data changes but the database itself works without streaming.
DynamoDB can store vector embeddings but requires external services or custom logic for vector search.
DynamoDB excels at key-value access patterns with partition keys providing fast lookups.
DynamoDB can model graph relationships using adjacency list patterns but is not optimized for graph queries.
DynamoDB can store time-series data with TTL support but is not specifically optimized for time-series workloads.
DynamoDB is optimized for OLTP workloads with consistent low latency and high throughput for transactional operations.
DynamoDB can export data to S3 for OLAP analysis but is primarily designed for OLTP workloads.

warn_nfr.reason: DynamoDB is a managed service with provisioning overhead and costs. At very low QPS (<5 req/s), simpler database solutions may be more cost-effective.
warn_nfr.message: db-nosql-document--dynamodb: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<5). DynamoDB's provisioning and cost model may not be optimal for very low throughput workloads; consider simpler alternatives.

warn_nfr.reason: DynamoDB write capacity units and costs are optimized for higher throughput scenarios. Very low write rates may not justify the service complexity.
warn_nfr.message: db-nosql-document--dynamodb: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (<2). DynamoDB's cost structure is optimized for higher write throughput; consider whether a simpler database would suffice.
